sprint: Lumo Voice — Full Batch: All 17 Lines Wired, Timing Synced to Real Recordings
task: Completes the one-line proof of concept from Sprint 2.81.0 into the full batch, per the user's own "we will update the text prompts in code in one go" instruction, once every remaining recording had actually arrived. Six previously-outstanding lines are now staged: the Returning Creator's recognition pair (Welcome home / Show me your stars), its reunion pair (It's good to see you again / Welcome back to your story), and the Creator Ceremony's two Guardian beats (A new light is stirring / I bless this Story Egg). js/lumoVoice.js's LINES map is rewritten from a bare id-to-filename lookup into id -> {file, ms}, where ms is each clip's own real, ffprobe-measured duration -- exposed synchronously via a new durationMs(ids). A new playSequence(ids) chains clips back to back off each one's own real 'ended' event (used for the Traveller greeting's title-then-subtitle pairs). A real timing problem was found before wiring anything blindly: several Traveller-greeting title+subtitle pairs run far longer than the existing fixed GREETING_LINE_MS(3400) -- one pair alone totals ~7.6s -- so js/gatewaySequence.js's playLines() gained an optional parallel voiceIds array and now computes each line's real effective hold as max(lineMs, LumoVoice.durationMs(voice)+400), stretching the on-screen bubble to genuinely fit the real recorded voice. Wired at all three real call sites (Traveller greeting, Returning Creator recognition, Returning Creator reunion) plus js/companionDirector.js's two Creator Ceremony Guardian beats (voiceId field) and js/magicCardUI.js's _playCeremonyBeats (LumoVoice.play(beat.voiceId)). Verified via a new 8-assertion Playwright suite driving all three real surfaces end-to-end through actual UI interaction. Full regression across the entire existing Gateway/Magic-Card Playwright suite passes unchanged, zero console errors; two pre-existing scratch tests needed their own fixed wait timeouts widened to match the new, correctly-longer, voice-synced total -- the intended, disclosed consequence of this fix, not a regression. Every one of Lumo's ~17 spoken lines in the app now has real recorded audio, correctly timed. No Theme Contract/Engine V2 Scene Model/Builder/Creator Governing Rules change; confined to js/lumoVoice.js, js/gatewaySequence.js, js/companionDirector.js, js/magicCardUI.js, and 6 new recorded audio files.
